extra.reasoning: The monotonicity property requires that flipping palpitations from false to true (all other inputs unchanged) never lowers acuity. Examining all rules r1-r7, the input variable palpitations does not appear as a condition in any rule (the rules trigger only on slurred_speech, focal_weakness, rash, back_pain, fever, chest_pain, and vomiting). Therefore palpitations has no influence whatsoever on the acuity output. For any fixed assignment of the other seven inputs, the set of matching rules, their priorities, the conflict resolution, and the default fallback all evaluate identically regardless of palpitations' value. Setting palpitations to true leaves acuity exactly equal to its prior value, which satisfies "no lower than before" (equality is allowed). The property holds across the entire input space.
